records.formulation_label: Saturated ibuprofen solution in 75:25 propylene glycol/water
records.formulation_name: Saturated ibuprofen solution in propylene glycol/water
records.api_concentration_raw: saturated solution (~10 mg/ml); solubility found to be 9.6 ± 0.7 mg/ml in 75:25 (v/v) propylene glycol/water
records.device: in vivo tape stripping / dermatopharmacokinetic experiment
records.membrane_source: ventral forearm of 5 human volunteers
records.dose_amount: 500 µL applied for 30 min under occlusive bandage
records.source_evidence: Results and Discussion, Figure 3 text: 'The integrated amounts in the SC were derived from the areas under the profiles in Figure 3 and yielded a mean (± SD) value of 12.2 (±3.5) µg/cm2.' Also Materials and Methods, in vivo DPK experiments - Ibuprofen
records.notes: This is an in vivo DPK/tape-stripping dataset, included because no in vitro permeation/release experiments are present in the paper. Endpoint at 0 h corresponds to immediate post-application AUC/amount in SC.

records.formulation_label: D6 HFMN array + lyophilised wafer-like reservoir
records.formulation_name: Hydrogel-forming microneedle array (D6) with lyophilised wafer-like reservoir
records.api_concentration_raw: IBU Na (40% w/w) in lyophilised wafer-like reservoir formulation
records.membrane_source: Neonatal porcine skin obtained from stillborn piglets; dermatomed to 350 μm
records.dose_amount: Approximately 100 mg IBU Na in lyophilised wafer-like reservoir; 20 μL PBS applied onto the centre of the HFMN array; stainless steel weight 11.0 g placed on top
records.source_evidence: Page 4, Methods 2.5.3 and Page 12, Fig. 9 / Results text: "Modified Franz diffusion cells were used... dermatomed (350 μm), neonatal porcine skin..."; "A 20 μl aliquot of PBS... to promote adhesion of the lyophilised wafer-like reservoir containing approximately 100 mg IBU Na..."; "D6 HFMN arrays... reaching approximately 32 ± 8% of the initial drug content ... at 24 h".
records.notes: Paper reports endpoint primarily as percent of initial drug content at 24 h; absolute mg permeated at 24 h not explicitly stated in text/figure.

records.notes: Paper reports endpoint primarily as percent of initial drug content at 24 h; absolute mg permeated at 24 h not explicitly stated in text/figure.

paper_scope.scope_notes: The paper contains one in vitro permeation experiment using modified Franz diffusion cells with hydrogel-forming microneedle arrays (D6 and D7) and ibuprofen sodium. No other in vitro permeation/release experiments were reported; dissolution/loading/deposition and insertion studies are not in scope.

title: Fluorinated PAMAM-Arginine Carrier Prodrugs for pH-Sensitive Sustained Ibuprofen Delivery.

Table I identifies compound 3 as PAMAM G2 hfVal-Arg 3.
records.notes: Exact numeric cell values were not tabulated in text; endpoint values were read from Fig. 3B. This formulation is a physical encapsulation system, not a skin permeation study.

paper_scope.scope_notes: The paper contains in vitro release experiments using dialysis bags/membranes for ibuprofen-loaded fluorinated PAMAM dendrimer complexes (3–6) and covalent ibuprofen-PAMAM prodrugs (1–2). No skin permeation/IVPT/franz-cell studies were reported.

paper_scope.scope_notes: The paper contains in vitro drug release experiments using a dialysis bag method for idebenone, ibuprofen, and miconazole formulations, plus corresponding neat drug suspension controls. No true skin IVPT/permeation/franz-cell membrane data were reported.

title: Design and development of a novel fused filament fabrication (FFF) 3D printed diffusion cell with UV imaging capabilities to characterise permeation in pharmaceutical formulations.

records.notes: Paper reports a single in vitro permeation experiment with triplicate run over 12 h; endpoint values are only presented graphically in Figure 11a, and the parsed text does not contain exact numeric y-values for each timepoint. Output includes a placeholder record for the study/formulation with endpoint value null at time 0 to preserve formulation scope; actual time-series numeric values are not explicitly readable in the provided text.

paper_scope.scope_notes: The paper contains one in vitro permeation/release experiment using a 3D printed Franz-like diffusion cell to measure ibuprofen permeation through a silicone membrane. No other in-scope formulations or separate experiments were described in tables/text; Figure 11 provides the only permeation time-course, but exact point values are not textually enumerated in the parsed content.

records.formulation_name: Water-treated control skin exposed to caffeine solution
records.api_concentration_raw: 10 µl of ethanol-water (1:1) solution of caffeine (1%)
records.membrane_source: freshly sacrificed domestic pigs (three donors), back skin
records.receptor_medium: 1% bovine serum albumin + 0.04% gentamicin sulfate in phosphate-buffered saline (pH 7.4) aqueous solution
records.dose_amount: 10 µl applied to entire skin surface after pretreatment; 90 µl pretreatment with water
records.source_evidence: Table 4 and Section 2.5 In vitro percutaneous assay; Table 4 lists RF = 3.22 ± 0.74 µg/cm2 and percutaneous absorption = 3.89 ± 0.95 for water-treated skin; conditions in Section 2.5 and 2.2.
records.notes: Endpoint recorded as receptor-fluid amount because table reports compartmental distribution; cumulative amount over 24 h not explicitly separated for this cell beyond compartment sum. Here the extracted value is the receptor fluid amount from Table 4.

records.formulation_name: SLS-pretreated skin exposed to caffeine solution
records.api_concentration_raw: 10 µl of ethanol-water (1:1) solution of caffeine (1%)
records.membrane_source: freshly sacrificed domestic pigs (three donors), back skin
records.receptor_medium: 1% bovine serum albumin + 0.04% gentamicin sulfate in phosphate-buffered saline (pH 7.4) aqueous solution
records.dose_amount: 10 µl applied to entire skin surface after pretreatment; 90 µl pretreatment with 1% SLS
records.source_evidence: Table 4 and Section 2.5; Table 4 lists RF = 25.45 ± 7.55 µg/cm2 and percutaneous absorption = 27.00 ± 7.64 for SLS-treated skin; Section 2.5 gives Franz cell and dose conditions.
records.notes: Extracted RF compartment amount. Table 4 also provides total percutaneous absorption 27.00 ± 7.64 µg/cm2.

records.formulation_name: Acetone-pretreated skin exposed to caffeine solution
records.api_concentration_raw: 10 µl of ethanol-water (1:1) solution of caffeine (1%)
records.membrane_source: freshly sacrificed domestic pigs (three donors), back skin
records.receptor_medium: 1% bovine serum albumin + 0.04% gentamicin sulfate in phosphate-buffered saline (pH 7.4) aqueous solution
records.dose_amount: 10 µl applied to entire skin surface after pretreatment; 90 µl pretreatment with acetone
records.source_evidence: Table 4 and Section 2.5; Table 4 lists RF = 1.65 ± 0.61 µg/cm2 and percutaneous absorption = 2.26 ± 0.72 for Ac-treated skin; Section 2.5 specifies 90 µl Ac pretreatment and 10 µl caffeine dosing.
records.notes: Extracted RF compartment amount. Table 4 also provides total percutaneous absorption 2.26 ± 0.72 µg/cm2.

records.formulation_name: Water-treated control skin exposed to ibuprofen solution
records.api_concentration_raw: 10 µl of ethanol-water (1:1) solution of ibuprofen (1%)
records.membrane_source: freshly sacrificed domestic pigs (three donors), back skin
records.dose_amount: 10 µl applied to entire skin surface after pretreatment; 90 µl pretreatment with water
records.source_evidence: Table 5 and Section 2.5; Table 5 lists RF = 7.56 ± 0.84 µg/cm2 and percutaneous absorption = 9.03 ± 1.18 for water-treated skin; Section 2.5 states ibuprofen receptor fluid was PBS pH 7.4 and dosing conditions.
records.notes: Extracted RF compartment amount. Table 5 also provides total percutaneous absorption 9.03 ± 1.18 µg/cm2.

records.formulation_name: SLS-pretreated skin exposed to ibuprofen solution
records.api_concentration_raw: 10 µl of ethanol-water (1:1) solution of ibuprofen (1%)
records.membrane_source: freshly sacrificed domestic pigs (three donors), back skin
records.dose_amount: 10 µl applied to entire skin surface after pretreatment; 90 µl pretreatment with 1% SLS
records.source_evidence: Table 5 and Section 2.5; Table 5 lists RF = 35.40 ± 10.78 µg/cm2 and percutaneous absorption = 37.05 ± 10.23 for SLS-treated skin; Section 2.5 provides pretreatment and receptor medium.
records.notes: Extracted RF compartment amount. Table 5 also provides total percutaneous absorption 37.05 ± 10.23 µg/cm2.

records.formulation_name: Acetone-pretreated skin exposed to ibuprofen solution
records.api_concentration_raw: 10 µl of ethanol-water (1:1) solution of ibuprofen (1%)
records.membrane_source: freshly sacrificed domestic pigs (three donors), back skin
records.dose_amount: 10 µl applied to entire skin surface after pretreatment; 90 µl pretreatment with acetone
records.source_evidence: Table 5 and Section 2.5; Table 5 lists RF = 11.19 ± 2.72 µg/cm2 and percutaneous absorption = 13.68 ± 2.78 for Ac-treated skin; Section 2.5 gives Ac pretreatment and ibuprofen receptor fluid conditions.
records.notes: Extracted RF compartment amount. Table 5 also provides total percutaneous absorption 13.68 ± 2.78 µg/cm2.

paper_scope.scope_notes: The paper contains in vitro Franz cell percutaneous absorption experiments for caffeine and ibuprofen on porcine skin under water, SLS, and acetone pretreatments. No separate release studies were reported.

excipient_composition.raw: PCL dissolved in 1:1 ratio of DCM and DMF

excipient_composition.raw: PCL dissolved in 1:1 ratio of DCM and DMF

records.membrane_source: fabricated in study
records.dose_amount: 1 cm × 1 cm fibrous mat suspended in 2 mL DPBS

paper_scope.scope_notes: Paper contains in vitro drug release experiments for ibuprofen-loaded electrospun PCL mats (single-layer and sandwich/tri-layer mats) measured over 62 days in DPBS pH 7.4. No in vitro skin permeation/Franz cell IVPT was reported.

title: Compatibilizing of cotton fabric with hydrophobic drug cover layer for anti-inflammatory performance with the implementation of ibuprofen.

paper_scope.scope_notes: The paper reports an in vitro ibuprofen release study from cotton textile dressings into PBS over 14 days (Table 4 and Figure 3). No skin permeation/Franz-cell IVPT study is reported; only release data are in scope.

title: Influence of critical parameters of nanosuspension formulation on the permeability of a poorly soluble drug through the skin--a case study.

paper_scope.scope_notes: Paper contains in vitro skin permeation experiments using static vertical Franz diffusion cells with dermatomed porcine skin and human skin. Main quantifiable in-scope data are Table II (porcine skin, 24/48/72 h cumulative amount and flux) and the human skin comparison in text/Fig. 4 (flux only). No separate in vitro release study is reported.

records.api_concentration_raw: saturated solution; concentration not explicitly reported
records.membrane_source: Landrace-Yorkshire hybrids from Dard Co., Ltd. (Tokyo, Japan)
records.dose_amount: 1.0 mL donor solution applied after 1-h acclimation
records.source_evidence: Methods: Preparation of Donor/Table I; Skin Permeation section; Fig. 2a and Table III. Skin: thickness approximately 1.0–1.2 mm. Cell: Franz diffusion cell, diffusion area 1.77 cm2, receptor PBS pH 7.4, 1.0 mL saturated donor solution. Quantitative endpoint from Fig. 2a (Q over 8 h, time course) and Table III (permeability parameters).
records.notes: Cumulative amount values were presented only graphically in Fig. 2a, not tabulated numerically in text. The formulation is the common vehicle saturated with each drug; concentration of the dissolved drug not explicitly reported.

records.api_concentration_raw: saturated solution; concentration not explicitly reported
records.membrane_source: Landrace-Yorkshire hybrids from Dard Co., Ltd. (Tokyo, Japan)
records.dose_amount: 1.0 mL donor solution applied after 1-h acclimation
records.source_evidence: Results text discussing AMP: "4.70-, 5.81-, and 4.84-fold higher permeation was observed for ANP, AMP, and MP, respectively, at T48RH95 compared with that at T27RH55" and Fig. 5 (log P at T48RH95 vs T27RH55). Since no numeric AMP table is shown, the flux/permeability values are not explicitly tabulated; P at T48RH95 is inferred from Fig. 5 axis and the enhancement ratio from text. The paper explicitly reports that the study used Franz diffusion cells with excised porcine skin and 1.0 mL donor solution.
records.notes: This record captures the AMP permeability coefficient at T48RH95 as approximated from Fig. 5 because the exact numeric value is not listed in a table. Endpoint value is estimated from the plotted log P (approximately 10^-6.74 cm/s).

records.api_concentration_raw: saturated solution; concentration not explicitly reported
records.membrane_source: Landrace-Yorkshire hybrids from Dard Co., Ltd. (Tokyo, Japan)
records.dose_amount: 1.0 mL donor solution applied after 1-h acclimation
records.source_evidence: Fig. 5 and accompanying text comparing log P at T48RH95 and T27RH55 for AMP. The paper reports 5.81-fold higher permeation for AMP at T48RH95 versus T27RH55, with donor/device conditions described in Methods.
records.notes: The paper reports comparative enhancement ratio for AMP, but not numeric cumulative amount values in text/table.

records.api_concentration_raw: saturated solution; concentration not explicitly reported
records.membrane_source: Landrace-Yorkshire hybrids from Dard Co., Ltd. (Tokyo, Japan)
records.dose_amount: 1.0 mL donor solution applied after 1-h acclimation
records.source_evidence: Table III reports ANP permeability parameter at T52RH30 as log P = -7.79 ± 0.046, which corresponds to P ≈ 1.62×10^-8 cm/s, and the text notes 4.70-, 3.73-, and 1.70-fold enhancement ratios for ANP across T48RH95/T50RH55/T52RH30 relative to T27RH55. This record is for the T52RH30 condition, but the exact enhancement ratio is only summarized in text.
records.notes: Endpoint value converted from log P reported in Table III: 10^-7.79 ≈ 1.62e-8 cm/s. No direct cumulative amount table.

records.notes: Figure 5 provides comparative log P only; no exact MP cumulative amount is tabulated.

paper_scope.scope_notes: The paper contains in vitro Caco-2 permeability/transcellular transport experiments for ten compounds with time-course sampling and calculated permeability parameters. No dermal/transdermal skin or membrane release studies are present; the in-scope records are the Caco-2 permeability assay records.

paper_scope.scope_notes: Paper contains in vitro membrane permeation experiments using a silicone membrane in a horizontal two-chamber diffusion cell. No skin, animal, human DPK, or tape-stripping studies were reported. Extracted all formulation-timepoint cumulative time-course records where the paper provided them via Fig. 3, plus steady-state flux values from text.

It does not report dermal/transdermal permeation, Franz cell, IVPT skin permeation, membrane diffusion, or topical release from a dosage form across a membrane. Therefore there are no in-scope permeation/release records under the requested dermal/transdermal criteria.

paper_scope.scope_notes: No in vitro permeation, diffusion cell, Franz cell, IVPT, transdermal release, or membrane/skin permeation experiments are reported in this paper. The paper focuses on phytochemical characterization and enzyme inhibition (α-amylase, α-glucosidase, hyaluronidase, lipoxygenase) of plant extracts processed by microfiltration/ultrafiltration, which are not in-scope permeation/release studies.
